heading: Considerations
body:
By default, the KMIP (Key Management Interoperability) Proxy Standalone uses the credentials stored in
the
`/<dbPath>/cloudProviderCredentials/<keyID>.<cloudProvider>.metadata`
file.

- If you rotate keys, these credentials reflect the latest key
  rotation.

- If the KMIP (Key Management Interoperability) Proxy Standalone binary is unable to decrypt the snapshots
  using these credentials, the binary shows an error message stating that
  you must update the metadata files on disk that contain the old
  credentials. You can update the metadata file with any text editor.

- If you use [role-based access to your encryption key](aws-kms-enable-project), the
  `/<dbPath>/cloudProviderCredentials/<keyID>.<cloudProvider>.metadata`
  file won't contain valid credentials.

  Take one of the following actions:

  - Update the
    `/<dbPath>/cloudProviderCredentials/<keyID>.<cloudProvider>.metadata`
    file. Use an empty `roleId`. Provide temporary credentials based
    on the IAM (Identity and Access Management) role that can access your encryption key in the
    `accessKeyId` and `secretAccessKey` fields:

    ```json
    {
      "accessKeyId": "TemporaryAccessKeyId",
      "secretAccessKey": "TemporarySecretAccessKey",
      "roleId": "",
      "region": "us-east-1"
    }
    ```

  - Start the KMIP (Key Management Interoperability) Proxy Standalone binary with the following options:

    - `awsAccessKey`

    - `awsSecretAccessKey`

    - `awsSessionToken`

    - `awsRegion`

  To generate temporary credentials based on an IAM (Identity and Access Management) role, see the
  [AWS documentation](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/STS/latest/APIReference/API_AssumeRole.html).

- You can download encrypted snapshots in the same way as
  unencrypted snapshots. We recommend using [role-based access to your encryption key](aws-kms-enable-project) for the project as a security best practice.
